pages.full_text: Lantern Vault of Bell Harry Tower, Canterbury Cathedral. By John Wastell 1503. Coats of Arms of Goldstone + Norton (Norton's Fork) The centre is a trap door above which is the large treadmill used in the construction of the tower.
photo_captions: Lantern Vault of Bell Harry Tower, Canterbury Cathedral. By John Wastell 1503. Coats of Arms of Goldstone + Norton (Norton's Fork) The centre is a trap door above which is the large treadmill used in the construction of the tower.
pages.notes: Page consists of a glued-in photograph of the vault ceiling of Bell Harry Tower at Canterbury Cathedral, with a handwritten caption below. No handwritten page number visible. 'Coats of Arms of Goldstone + Norton (Norton's Fork)' — Norton's Fork is a historical reference; 'Goldstone' could also read 'Goldstone' or similar. No year is present on this page; contextual placement in volume B near page 300 suggests circa 2018 based on the adjacent right-hand page's date inscription.

pages.full_text:
Camping van log April 13th 2018 – June 25th 2022

Sophie Allport

This book belongs to...
Simon + Eve Kirby

86 Westfield Rd, Edgbaston B15 3JG  U.K

Église abbatiale, choeur
pages.notes: This is the inside front cover / ownership page of a new journal (Sophie Allport branded notebook). The date range 'April 13th 2018 – June 25th 2022' inscribed at the top gives the span of the volume. 'This book belongs to...' is printed text on the label; 'Simon + Eve Kirby' and the address are handwritten. A small photo postcard of the Église abbatiale choeur at Abbaye Notre-Dame de Sénanque (Provence, France) is glued in, along with the abbey's printed logo/bookmark. The handwritten page number '1' may be faintly visible at the far left edge but is uncertain — recorded as null. 'Abbaye Notre-Dame de Sénanque' inferred from the printed logo text on the bookmark.
